fdh.sota_hypothesis: Anomaly score: z-score of TX_AMOUNT vs fraud-negative customer baseline (neg-only mean/std per customer). Captures transactions that are unusual even relative to the customer's legitimate spending patterns. 2 new features (anomaly_neg_zscore, anomaly_neg_zscore_log) on top of SOTA 35.

velocity features: rolling 1h/24h/7d counts+sums per customer and terminal, customer-terminal novelty, terminal recent 28d fraud rate, customer amount percentiles
velocity features: rolling 1h/24h/7d counts+sums per customer, 24h/7d per terminal; customer amount percentiles, terminal recent 28d fraud rate
within-split rolling velocity: customer 1h/24h/7d + terminal 24h/7d counts/sums; customer daily avg stats; terminal recent 28d fraud rate; customer amount percentiles

OOF target encoding customer+terminal, velocity stats from training (gap/burst/daily_rate), terminal multi-window fraud rates (7d/14d/28d), hour×terminal interaction fraud rate, customer amount range features

baseline + terminal multi-window fraud rates (7d/14d/28d), customer velocity stats (gap/burst/daily_rate), amount percentile flags (small/large for customer), customer unique terminals count, is_new_terminal
smoothed terminal/customer fraud rates, IQR-based amount anomaly, terminal fraud trend (7d/28d ratio), customer fraud rate, interaction feature (zscore*term_fraud_rate)
restore exp_005 SOTA features + vectorized cust_term_visit_count novelty (faster than apply); same feature set for stability check
model regularization: max_depth=5, min_child_weight=20, reg_alpha=0.5, reg_lambda=2.0, gamma=0.1, LR=0.03 — reduce overfitting to improve OOT generalization
LightGBM (CPU) with good regularization: num_leaves=63, min_child_samples=30, reg_alpha=0.5, reg_lambda=2.0, LR=0.03 — compare to XGBoost SOTA
add: IQR-based amount anomaly (continuous), is_very_large_for_cust (p95), hour deviation from customer's typical pattern, terminal amount z-score, terminal std_amt — stronger scenario 1+3 signals
add: IQR-based amount anomaly (continuous), is_very_large_for_cust (p95), hour deviation from customer's typical pattern, terminal amount z-score — stronger scenario 1+3 signals
model tuning: deeper model (max_depth=6), lighter regularization (min_child_weight=15, reg_alpha=0.3), more trees (2000) — same features as exp_008 SOTA
more regularized XGBoost: max_depth=4, min_child_weight=30, reg_alpha=1.0, reg_lambda=3.0, gamma=0.2 — reduce overfitting for better OOT
XGBoost: max_depth=5, min_child_weight=25, reg_alpha=0.8, reg_lambda=2.5, gamma=0.15, LR=0.025 — intermediate regularization between SOTA and exp_013
XGBoost: max_depth=4, LR=0.03, min_child_weight=25, reg_alpha=0.8, reg_lambda=2.5, gamma=0.15 — max_depth=4 with original LR
XGBoost: max_depth=5, LR=0.03, min_child_weight=25, reg_alpha=0.7, reg_lambda=2.5, gamma=0.1 — slightly less regularization than exp_013, same depth as SOTA
max_depth=4 model + amount_ratio_cust_p95 and amount_ratio_cust_max features (scenario 1: high-amount fraud signal)
max_depth=4 (same as exp_013 that got OOT=0.211) with slightly softer regularization (min_child_weight=20, reg_alpha=0.5, gamma=0.05) to push val above threshold
ensemble: 2 XGBoost models (max_depth=4, seeds 42+123) averaged — variance reduction should push composite above SOTA threshold
3-model ensemble: depth=4 seed=42, depth=4 seed=123, depth=5 seed=7 — more diversity than 2-model ensemble
4-model ensemble: depth=4, seeds=[42,123,256,789] — more seed diversity for better variance reduction
add cust_terminal_diversity (unique_terminals/total_txns) for scenario 3 — card-not-present customers visit many different terminals; 2-model ensemble SOTA
confirm 2-model ensemble SOTA reproducibility with exp_019 features — stability check
2-model ensemble (seeds 42+123, depth=4) with slightly higher subsample/colsample (0.80 vs 0.75) — less variance in tree construction
2-model ensemble with colsample_bylevel=0.75 for more node-level randomness — may improve OOT generalization

Feature ablation: prune 22 dead-weight features, keep only top 13 by importance (term_fraud_rate, amount_vs_cust_median_ratio, is_large_for_cust, term_fr_28d, amount_cust_zscore, etc). Cleaner input matrix should improve generalization and break the 6-discard streak.

Rolling velocity features: within-split counts of txns in past 1h and 6h per CUSTOMER_ID and TERMINAL_ID, capturing card-not-present burst patterns and terminal compromise scenarios. O(N log N) searchsorted implementation.

Cyclic time encoding: add sin/cos of hour-of-day and day-of-week on top of SOTA features. Cyclic encoding captures periodicity better than raw integers (midnight=23 is close to 0), especially for is_night fraud patterns.

Interaction features: multiply top signals (term_fraud_rate × is_large_for_cust, term_fraud_rate × amount_cust_zscore, term_fraud_rate × amount_vs_cust_median_ratio) to create explicit combined fraud indicators; also add term_fr_trend (28d vs overall) and new_term_x_term_fraud.

Interaction features (5): term_fraud_rate x is_large_for_cust, term_fraud_rate x amount_cust_zscore, term_fraud_rate x amount_vs_cust_median_ratio, new_terminal x term_fraud, term_fr_28d vs all-time (trend). Explicit combinations of top signals within 40-feature limit.

Interaction features (4): term_fraud_rate x is_large_for_cust, term_fraud_rate x amount_cust_zscore, term_fraud_rate x amount_vs_cust_median_ratio, is_new_terminal x term_fraud_rate. Explicit products of top fraud signals to amplify combined detection.

Anomaly score: z-score of TX_AMOUNT vs fraud-negative customer baseline (neg-only mean/std per customer). Captures transactions that are unusual even relative to the customer's legitimate spending patterns. 2 new features (anomaly_neg_zscore, anomaly_neg_zscore_log) on top of SOTA 35.

Extend anomaly breakthrough: add terminal-level negative-baseline anomaly (same neg-only zscore idea applied to TERMINAL_ID). Customer neg zscore was #3-4 importance at 28% combined. Terminal version should capture unusual amounts vs that terminal's legitimate patterns.

Extend anomaly score: add neg-baseline median ratio (TX_AMOUNT / negative-only customer median). Complements existing anomaly_neg_zscore (which uses mean). Median is more robust to outliers and may capture different signal. SOTA has anomaly_neg_zscore at 28% combined importance.

Build on anomaly breakthrough: add is_above_neg_p90 (binary flag: TX_AMOUNT > 90th percentile of customer negative history). Cleaner signal than continuous zscore - directly answers 'is this amount in top 10% of what this customer normally spends legitimately?'

fraud-sim.sota_hypothesis: add amount distribution shape: card_amt_skew, amt_vs_card_q90_ratio, amt_above_card_q90, amt_pct_in_card_range; add is_weekend time feature

city population features: log_city_pop, city_pop_zscore, is_small_city, is_rural, city_pop_ratio vs card home city

per-card-category shopping frequency fraction, merchant repeat-visitor ratio, hour/DOW target encoding
pruned feature set: removed velocity_std_gap/gap_q25/gap_q75/min_gap/is_high_velocity, behav_amt_above_median/is_max_ever, redundant velocity logs — leaner model
per-card behavioral Mahalanobis anomaly: distance from each card's OWN behavioral centroid (log_amt + hour), not the global centroid; card_ba_mahalanobis, card_ba_amt_zscore, card_ba_hour_zscore
cyclic time encoding: add hour_sin, hour_cos, dow_sin, dow_cos alongside raw hour and day_of_week to capture circular wraparound at midnight and weekend
cyclic time encoding: add hour_sin, hour_cos, dow_sin, dow_cos alongside raw hour and day_of_week to capture circular wraparound at midnight and weekend
terminal/merchant risk (Recipe 14): smoothed Bayesian fraud rate per merchant, term_txn_volume, term_is_high_risk flag — merchant is the terminal in fraud-sim
baseline verification: restored SOTA features, adding haversine geo distance from card home lat/long to merchant merch_lat/merch_long (Recipe 13) — key untried geo signal

haversine geo distance (Recipe 13): distance from card home centroid to merchant (geo_dist_home_to_merch_km) and current transaction lat to merchant (geo_dist_txn_km); log transform and far_from_home flag

per-card geo behavioral anomaly: compute txn distance (customer lat to merchant), compare against card's typical travel range (median, std, max); geo_dist_zscore and geo_dist_ratio_max detect unusually distant transactions for this specific card

card merchant/category diversity: card_n_unique_merchants, card_n_unique_categories, card_merch_concentration (unique merchants / total txns) — fraud cards often show unusual concentration or diversity patterns vs legitimate cards

stateless interaction features: amt_x_is_night, behav_max_ratio_x_night, rare_cat_high_amt (1-category_freq) * behav_amt_zscore, double_anomaly_score (hour_deviation * amt_ratio_max) — explicit cross-products of top features to capture joint fraud patterns

feature ablation: remove low-importance features — drop velocity_std_gap/min_gap, merchant/gender amt deviation, state/job target encoding, behav_amt_above_median/is_max_ever — reduce from 52 to ~38 features to improve signal-to-noise ratio

terminal risk (Recipe 14, min_samples=100 heavy smoothing) + stateless haversine geo distance (lat/long to merch_lat/merch_long) — combining merchant fraud rate signal with geographic distance signal, both stable across population shift

combined: terminal merchant fraud rate (Recipe 14, min_samples=100) + stateless geo distance + per-card geo behavioral anomaly (geo_dist_zscore, geo_is_farther_than_usual) + card merchant diversity (n_unique_merchants, merch_concentration) — stacking multiple complementary signals for a larger combined boost
